She is the first woman to hold the office in the country’s history.

President of Slovenia since 2022

In this article, the [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Pirc Musar*.

**Nataša Pirc Musar** ([née](/wiki/Birth_name#Maiden_and_married_names "Birth name") **Pirc**; born May 9, 1968) is a Slovenian lawyer, author, and politician who has served as the [President of Slovenia](/wiki/President_of_Slovenia "President of Slovenia") since December 2022. She is the first woman to hold the office in the country’s history.

Prior to her presidency, Pirc Musar served as Slovenia’s [Information Commissioner](/wiki/Information_commissioner "Information commissioner") from 2004 to 2014, where she became widely recognized for defending [freedom of information](/wiki/Freedom_of_information "Freedom of information") and [privacy law](/wiki/Privacy_law "Privacy law"). She later founded her own law firm, representing a number of high-profile clients, including Slovenian-born [First Lady of the United States](/wiki/First_Lady_of_the_United_States "First Lady of the United States") [Melania Trump](/wiki/Melania_Trump "Melania Trump") and members of the Slovenian [Social Democrats](/wiki/Social_Democrats_(Slovenia) "Social Democrats (Slovenia)"). From 2015 to 2016, she also served as president of the [Slovenian Red Cross](/wiki/Slovenian_Red_Cross "Slovenian Red Cross").

Politically, Pirc Musar is considered an [independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") and [liberal](/wiki/Liberalism "Liberalism")-leaning figure who emphasizes human rights, the [rule of law](/wiki/Rule_of_law "Rule of law"), transparency, and strong democratic institutions. Although not formally affiliated with a political party, she has received support from several [centre-left](/wiki/Centre-left_politics "Centre-left politics") and [pro-European](/wiki/Pro-Europeanism "Pro-Europeanism") groups.

In the second round of the [2022 presidential election](/wiki/2022_Slovenian_presidential_election "2022 Slovenian presidential election"), she defeated [Anže Logar](/wiki/An%C5%BEe_Logar "Anže Logar") of the [Slovenian Democratic Party](/wiki/Slovenian_Democratic_Party "Slovenian Democratic Party") and was elected President of Slovenia.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nata%C5%A1a_Pirc_Musar&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Born in [Ljubljana](/wiki/Ljubljana "Ljubljana"), Pirc Musar studied law at the [University of Ljubljana](/wiki/University_of_Ljubljana "University of Ljubljana")'s Faculty of Law until 1992, where [Marko Ilešič](/wiki/Marko_Ile%C5%A1i%C4%8D "Marko Ilešič"), later a judge at the [European Court of Justice](/wiki/European_Court_of_Justice "European Court of Justice"), was her supervisor.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) In 1997, she passed the bar exam, and later started working at the [Radio-Television Slovenia](/wiki/Radiotelevizija_Slovenija "Radiotelevizija Slovenija"), where she worked for six years as a journalist and host of the central news program.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) Later, for five years, she was the presenter of the central news program *[24UR](/wiki/24UR "24UR")* on the commercial television channel [POP TV](/wiki/Pop_(Slovenian_TV_channel) "Pop (Slovenian TV channel)").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) In 2001, she became the head of the corporate communication department at Aktiva Group, where her husband Aleš Musar worked.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Pirc Musar completed additional training at [CNN](/wiki/CNN "CNN") in [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta"), Georgia.[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) She then continued her studies for two semesters at the [University of Salford](/wiki/University_of_Salford "University of Salford") in England, during which she did internships at the [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC"), [Granada TV](/wiki/ITV_Granada "ITV Granada"), [Sky News](/wiki/Sky_News "Sky News"), [Reuters TV](/wiki/Reuters_TV "Reuters TV"), and [Border TV](/wiki/ITV_Border "ITV Border").[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) In 2015 she obtained a PhD at the [University of Vienna](/wiki/University_of_Vienna "University of Vienna")'s Faculty of Law with a dissertation on a fair balance between [privacy rights](/wiki/Privacy_rights "Privacy rights") and the [freedom of information](/wiki/Freedom_of_information "Freedom of information").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:4-6)

## Legal and business career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nata%C5%A1a_Pirc_Musar&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Legal and business career")]

In April 2003, Pirc Musar joined the [Supreme Court of Slovenia](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_Slovenia "Supreme Court of Slovenia") as director of the Center for Education and Information.[[6]](#cite_note-:4-6) Pirc Musar is best remembered for being the Commissioner for Access to Public Information between 2004 and 2014.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) From March 2011, she was the vice-president of the Joint Supervisory Body for [Europol](/wiki/Europol "Europol"), and from 2012 until the end of her term as information commissioner, she was the president of this body of the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

After the end of her term as information commissioner, she founded her own law firm.[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9) Rosana Lemut Strle, became a partner in the law firm in 2016, and the Law Firm is now called Pirc Musar & Lemut Strle.[[9]](#cite_note-:5-9) Among others she represented [Melania Trump](/wiki/Melania_Trump "Melania Trump"), during her husband's US presidency.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) In highly publicized cases she represented politicians of the [Social Democrats](/wiki/Social_Democrats_(Slovenia) "Social Democrats (Slovenia)"),[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13) the ambassador to the United States Stanislav Vidovič,[[14]](#cite_note-14) among others.

Between 2010 and 2021, Pirc Musar was voted among the top ten most influential lawyers in the country numerous times.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18) She co-founded the OnaVe association to connect female experts and promote knowledge.[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-demokracija.si-20) From 2015 to 2016, she was the president of the Slovenian Red Cross.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

Pirc Musar has authored or co-authored at least six books on freedom of information and privacy in Slovenian, English, and Croatian.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-Maribor-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-plus.cobiss.net-27)

## Political career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nata%C5%A1a_Pirc_Musar&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Political career")]

### President of Slovenia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nata%C5%A1a_Pirc_Musar&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: President of Slovenia")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7b/Otvoritvena_slovesnost_Evropske_prestolnice_kulture%2C_Nova_Gorica%2C_8._2._2025_157.jpg/250px-Otvoritvena_slovesnost_Evropske_prestolnice_kulture%2C_Nova_Gorica%2C_8._2._2025_157.jpg)](/wiki/File:Otvoritvena_slovesnost_Evropske_prestolnice_kulture,_Nova_Gorica,_8._2._2025_157.jpg)

Musar and Italian President [Sergio Mattarella](/wiki/Sergio_Mattarella "Sergio Mattarella") in February 2025

On June 23, 2022, Pirc Musar announced her candidacy for [President of Slovenia](/wiki/President_of_Slovenia "President of Slovenia") in the [2022 Slovenian presidential election](/wiki/2022_Slovenian_presidential_election "2022 Slovenian presidential election"), scheduled for October 23, 2022, as an independent candidate.[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29) She was the first to announce her candidacy for president, and she was endorsed by former presidents of Slovenia [Milan Kučan](/wiki/Milan_Ku%C4%8Dan "Milan Kučan") and [Danilo Türk](/wiki/Danilo_T%C3%BCrk "Danilo Türk").[[30]](#cite_note-30) Pirc Musar has not been a member of a political party, and she does not plan to become one.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] While she defended her candidacy as an independent, parties such as the [Pirate Party](/wiki/Pirate_Party_(Slovenia) "Pirate Party (Slovenia)")[[31]](#cite_note-:3-31) and [Youth Party – European Greens](/wiki/Youth_Party_%E2%80%93_European_Greens "Youth Party – European Greens") also supported her.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Her candidacy sparked media speculation about her relationship with [Marta Kos](/wiki/Marta_Kos "Marta Kos"), vice president of the ruling party [Freedom Movement](/wiki/Freedom_Movement_(Slovenia) "Freedom Movement (Slovenia)"), who announced her own candidacy for president a little later.[[32]](#cite_note-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33) Pirc Musar and Kos claimed to be friends, but according to media reports they stopped communicating with each other.[[20]](#cite_note-demokracija.si-20) In September 2022, Kos withdrew her candidacy, which led to a surge in support for Pirc Musar, who was already leading.[[34]](#cite_note-:2-34) In October, she came second in the first round of the presidential elections, therefore becoming one of the two contenders in the run-off of the presidential elections in November 2022.[[35]](#cite_note-:7-35) In the second round of November 13, Pirc Musar defeated [Slovenian Democratic Party](/wiki/Slovenian_Democratic_Party "Slovenian Democratic Party") candidate [Anže Logar](/wiki/An%C5%BEe_Logar "Anže Logar") and was elected Slovenia's first female president.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

In September 2025, Musar criticized [world powers](/wiki/Great_power "Great power") and permanent members of the [Security Council](/wiki/Security_Council "Security Council") (Russia, China, the United States, France, and the United Kingdom) for only pursuing their narrow national interests and offering "terror, conflict, pollution, fear, inequalities and war" instead of defending peace. She also called for an end to the [genocide in Gaza](/wiki/Gaza_genocide "Gaza genocide"), saying, "We did not stop the Holocaust, we did not stop the genocide in Rwanda, we did not stop the genocide in Srebrenica. We must stop the genocide in Gaza. There are no excuses any more, none!"[[37]](#cite_note-37)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nata%C5%A1a_Pirc_Musar&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Pirc Musar is married to Aleš Musar, a businessman. They have a son. Her husband is the owner of a property known as the "Russian dacha" in [Zgornje Gameljne](/wiki/Zgornje_Gameljne "Zgornje Gameljne").[[38]](#cite_note-38) The couple owns a [Rolls-Royce Phantom VI](/wiki/Rolls-Royce_Phantom_VI "Rolls-Royce Phantom VI") limousine, made in 1971 for and once owned by [Princess Alexandra](/wiki/Princess_Alexandra,_The_Honourable_Lady_Ogilvy "Princess Alexandra, The Honourable Lady Ogilvy"), first cousin of [Queen Elizabeth II](/wiki/Elizabeth_II "Elizabeth II").[[39]](#cite_note-39)[[40]](#cite_note-40)
